html:
Exclusive to:

The Lunar Siphonator, also known as the completed form of the Incomplete Experiment, is a craftable structure exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them.

The Incomplete Experiment Blueprint will be given to players by Wagstaff upon entering a Moonstorm for the first time, along with the blueprint for the Astroggles. The Blueprint can also be crafted at the Notional Fabricator on the Hermit Island.

The Incomplete Experiment serves as the first stage of the Lunar Siphonator and can only be built over the Mysterious Energy spawned by assembling the Celestial Altar, Celestial Sanctum and Celestial Tribute nearby to one another.

Similar to the Hermit Home, it is crafted in stages by adding materials for each upgrade:

  • Stage 1 requires crafting the Lunar Siphonator with 4 Scrap, 5 Moongleams and 2 Electrical Doodads, then placing it on the Mysterious Energy.
  • Stage 2 requires 4 Scrap, 10 Moongleams and 10 Infused Moon Shards.
  • Stage 3 requires one Restrained Static, the Celestial Orb and 20 Infused Moon Shards. This completes the Lunar Siphonator.

Therefore, the total cost of building the Lunar Siphonator is:

  • 1 Restrained Static: Reward for helping Wagstaff complete his experiment.
  • 8 Scrap: Can be obtained from Junk Pile and Junky Fence
  • 15 Moongleams: Caught with Bug Nets during the Moonstorm Event
  • 30 Infused Shards: Mined during the Moonstorm event
  • 2 Electrical Doodads
  • The Celestial Orb: Obtained by mining the Suspicious Boulder. The boulder is guaranteed to spawn during the next Meteor Shower after day 60, though it's possible for it to spawn earlier.

Immediately after the completion of Stage 3, meteors will crash down around the structure, which will destroy it and spawn the Celestial Champion.

Trivia

  • The Lunar Siphonator and the Incomplete Experiment were introduced in the Eye of the Storm update.

Gallery

  • Lunar Siphonator on the floor
  • Lunar Siphonator at the end of the Eye of the Storm Update Trailer.
  • The Lunar Siphonator silhouette image as a teaser is slightly different from the official version.
  • The Lunar Siphonator image as a teaser is slightly different from the official version.
  • Concept art of Lunar Essence Extractor or Lunar Siphonator from Rhymes With Play #280
  • Lunar Siphonator animations test from Rhymes With Play #280.
  • Lunar Siphonator Concept Art from Rhymes With Play #280.
  • Lunar Siphonator animations test from Rhymes With Play #280.

  • Lunar Siphonator test from Rhymes With Play #280.

"moon_device_construction3"
“It's containing the energy! I knew what it was for all along, of course.”
–Wilson, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“Great! So what does it do?”
–Willow, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“Wolfgang understand, is giant night light!”
–Wolfgang, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“How very peculiar.”
–Wendy, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“DON'T TRUST HIM, SISTER. DON'T EVER TRUST HIM”
–WX-78, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“As I suspected, it's meant to function as a battery of sorts.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“Huh. Whattaya figure it's for, Luce?”
–Woodie, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“That old fool...”
–Maxwell, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“Mere mörtals were nöt meant tö tamper sö with the pöwer öf the göds...”
–Wigfrid, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“It looks pretty impressive... whatever it is!”
–Webber, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“Ah, so that's what it's for."”
–Warly, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“Oooooh!”
–Wormwood, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“Wonder what the boss is gonna use all this energy for?”
–Winona, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“Hyuyuyu, I don't think they'll like that...”
–Wortox, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“Have weird feeling, florp...”
–Wurt, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“Wow! I wonder what it's for?”
–Walter, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
“It's finished...”
–Wanda, when examining the completed Lunar Siphonator.
The Lunar Siphonator, also known as the completed form of the Incomplete Experiment, is a craftable structure exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them.

Similar to the Hermit Home, it is crafted in stages by adding materials for each upgrade:
Stage 1 requires crafting the Lunar Siphonator with 4 Scrap, 5 Moongleams and 2 Electrical Doodads, then placing it on the Mysterious Energy.
Stage 2 requires 4 Scrap, 10 Moongleams and 10 Infused Moon Shards.
Stage 3 requires one Restrained Static, the Celestial Orb and 20 Infused Moon Shards. This completes the Lunar Siphonator.
Therefore, the total cost of building the Lunar Siphonator is:

8 Scrap: Can be obtained from Junk Pile and Junky Fence
15 Moongleams: Caught with Bug Nets during the Moonstorm Event

Immediately after the completion of Stage 3, meteors will crash down around the structure, which will destroy it and spawn the Celestial Champion.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Lunar Siphonator, còn được gọi là dạng hoàn chỉnh của Thử nghiệm chưa hoàn chỉnh, là một cấu trúc có thể chế tạo được dành riêng cho Don't Starve Together, được giới thiệu trong Return of Them.

The '''Lunar Siphonator''', also known as the completed form of the '''Incomplete Experiment''', is a craftable structure exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''.

The Incomplete Experiment [[Blueprint]] will be given to players by [[Grainy Transmission|Wagstaff]] upon entering a [[Moonstorm]] for the first time, along with the blueprint for the [[Astroggles]]. The Blueprint can also be crafted at the [[Notional Fabricator]] on the [[Hermit Island]].

The Incomplete Experiment serves as the first stage of the Lunar Siphonator and can only be built over the [[Mysterious Energy]] spawned by assembling the [[Celestial Altar]], [[Celestial Sanctum]] and [[Celestial Tribute]] nearby to one another.

Similar to the [[Hermit Home]], it is crafted in stages by adding materials for each upgrade:

* Stage 1 requires crafting the Lunar Siphonator with 4 [[Scrap]], 5 [[Moongleam]]s and 2 [[Electrical Doodad]]s, then placing it on the [[Mysterious Energy]].
* Stage 2 requires 4 Scrap, 10 Moongleams and 10 [[Infused Moon Shard]]s.
* Stage 3 requires one [[Restrained Static]], the [[Celestial Orb]] and 20 Infused Moon Shards. This completes the Lunar Siphonator.

Therefore, the total cost of building the Lunar Siphonator is:

* 1 Restrained Static: Reward for helping Wagstaff complete his experiment.
* 8 Scrap: Can be obtained from [[Junk Pile]] and [[Junky Fence]]
* 15 Moongleams: Caught with [[Bug Net]]s during the Moonstorm Event
* 30 Infused Shards: Mined during the Moonstorm event
* 2 Electrical Doodads
* The Celestial Orb: Obtained by mining the [[Suspicious Boulder]]. The boulder is guaranteed to spawn during the next [[Meteor|Meteor Shower]] after day 60, though it's possible for it to spawn earlier.

Immediately after the completion of Stage 3, meteors will crash down around the structure, which will destroy it and spawn the [[Celestial Champion]].

== {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* The Lunar Siphonator and the Incomplete Experiment were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Eye of the Storm|Eye of the Storm]]'' update.
